description: Central registry of Splunkbase app metadata and deployment-role placement for this repo. Used by the cloud installer, batch scripts, tests, and generated deployment docs as the single source of truth for package identity and role support.

deployment_role_descriptions.search-tier: Search heads or search-head clusters that host app UI, REST handlers, dashboards, macros, and search-time knowledge objects.
deployment_role_descriptions.indexer: Indexing tier where index-time parsing or indexer-side knowledge objects belong.
deployment_role_descriptions.heavy-forwarder: Customer-managed full Splunk Enterprise instance used for data collection, parsing, or forwarding.
deployment_role_descriptions.universal-forwarder: Lightweight forwarder tier used where the package or workflow is explicitly forwarder-safe.
deployment_role_descriptions.external-collector: Non-Splunk runtime such as SC4S, SC4SNMP, containers, or Kubernetes workloads that send data into Splunk.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `cisco-catalyst-ta-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: REST via custom account/input handlers
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Cisco EULA license-ack required.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `cisco-catalyst-enhanced-netflow-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: Manual install on HF/UF you control
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: No app-local config; validate the NetFlow/IPFIX receiver path on the same target
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Cisco EULA license-ack required. Optional forwarder-side mappings for extra Enterprise Networking NetFlow dashboards.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `cisco-appdynamics-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: REST via custom account/input handlers
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Configure the AppDynamics controller account and data inputs after install.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `cisco-security-cloud-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: REST via CiscoSecurityCloud custom admin handlers
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Cisco EULA license-ack required. Multi-product Cisco Security integration app with product-specific setup flows for each packaged integration.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `cisco-secure-access-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: REST via `org_accounts`, `update_settings`, and related app APIs
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Cisco EULA license-ack required. Secure Access automation installs both the event add-on and dashboard app, then covers org onboarding plus dashboard settings/bootstrap.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `cisco-secure-access-setup` add-on
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Add-on event inputs and log-index settings
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Cisco EULA license-ack required. Required companion add-on for Secure Access, Umbrella, and Cloudlock event-log ingestion.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `cisco-webex-setup` Webex add-on
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: REST via Webex add-on account/input handlers for OAuth accounts and Webex REST API inputs
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Installs with the companion Webex App dashboards. Covers meetings, admin/security audit, meeting quality, detailed call history, generic endpoint, and Contact Center Search inputs.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `cisco-webex-setup` Webex app
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Search-tier macro alignment for wx, wxc, and wxcc dashboard indexes
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Companion dashboard app for the Webex Add-on. Provides Webex Meetings, Calling, Contact Center, Security, and data-quality dashboards.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `cisco-ucs-ta-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: REST via UCS server, template, and cisco_ucs_task handlers
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Creates the cisco_ucs index, configures UCS Manager server records with encrypted passwords, default/custom class-ID templates, and cisco:ucs inputs.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `cisco-secure-email-web-gateway-setup` ESA
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Search-tier/indexer parser package plus email index and Cisco_ESA_Index macro alignment
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Parser/search-time add-on. Collection is SC4S or file monitor; this repo prepares Splunk-side package placement, indexes, macros, and handoff snippets.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `cisco-secure-email-web-gateway-setup` WSA
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Search-tier/indexer parser package plus netproxy index and Cisco_WSA_Index macro alignment
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Parser/search-time add-on. Collection is SC4S or file monitor; this repo prepares Splunk-side package placement, indexes, macros, and handoff snippets.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `cisco-talos-intelligence-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase for supported ES Cloud stacks
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Validate ES Cloud support posture, service-account fingerprint, get_talos_enrichment capability, alert actions, and threatlist state
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Enterprise Security Cloud-first integration. Service account certificate/private key is normally Splunk Cloud-provisioned and remains file-based for diagnostics only.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `cisco-meraki-ta-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: REST via custom account/input handlers
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Cisco EULA license-ack required. Dashboard macro alignment after install.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `cisco-intersight-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: REST via custom account/input handlers
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Cisco EULA license-ack required. Index creation uses ACS.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `cisco-dc-networking-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: REST via custom account/input handlers
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Cisco EULA license-ack required. Index creation uses ACS.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `cisco-enterprise-networking-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: REST for macros/saved searches/datamodel settings
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Cisco EULA license-ack required. Visualization app only; installer auto-adds required TA `7538` when missing. Optional Enhanced Netflow add-on `6872` should be offered separately when users want extra NetFlow dashboards.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `cisco-product-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: Delegates to the routed Cisco app install path for the resolved product
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Delegates to the routed Cisco setup skill after resolving the SCAN catalog product name
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Top-level Cisco product orchestrator. Uses the packaged SCAN catalog plus local overrides to resolve a product name, classify gaps, and run the matching setup workflow.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `cisco-scan-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Search-tier REST validation and optional S3 catalog sync
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Cisco EULA license-ack required. Search-head-only catalog app; no data inputs, indexes, or forwarder deployment.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `cisco-thousandeyes-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: REST for OAuth account, HEC-based streaming inputs, polling inputs
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Requires HEC token. OAuth device code flow for auth. ITSI integration optional.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-itsi-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: No post-install REST config needed
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Premium product; requires ITSI license. Enables ThousandEyes ITSI integration.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-itsi-config` content library
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase or Splunk Support / Cloud App Request
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Content pack catalog refresh, preview, install, and validate through ITSI REST workflows
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Requires ITSI app 1841. Used by splunk-itsi-config for supported content-pack workflows.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-enterprise-security-install`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: Splunk Support coordinated ES service workflow
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: `splunk-enterprise-security-config` validation plus Splunk Support for Cloud-managed indexer-side changes
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Premium SIEM product. On-prem installs prefer the local ES package when present, fall back to Splunkbase app 263, then run essinstall.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-enterprise-security-config`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: No additional app install; requires ES already provisioned
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: REST validation and supported search-tier settings; Splunk Cloud indexer-side changes remain support-managed
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Operational ES configuration workflow for indexes, roles, CIM data models, enrichment, detections, risk, and validation.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-security-portfolio-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: Delegates to the routed Splunk security product install path for the resolved product
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Delegates to the routed setup, config, or explicit handoff workflow after resolving the security product catalog entry
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Top-level Splunk security product router/audit workflow covering Enterprise Security, Security Essentials, SOAR, UBA, Attack Analyzer, Asset and Risk Intelligence, and associated security offerings.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-security-essentials-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Search-tier validation plus product UI checklist for Data Inventory Introspection, Content Mapping, app configuration, and optional posture dashboards
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Search-head-only app. Safe to install alongside Enterprise Security and has no indexer impact.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-asset-risk-intelligence-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase or Cloud app request for restricted entitlement; local package fallback when provided
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: ACS/REST index readiness for ari_staging, ari_asset, ari_internal, and ari_ta plus KV Store, roles/capabilities, post-install configuration, data source, metric, response/audit, investigation, ES integration, Add-on, Echo, upgrade, and uninstall-prerequisite handoffs
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Restricted Asset and Risk Intelligence app. Normal ES integration covers asset/identity sync and risk factors; ES 8.5+ Exposure Analytics routes to splunk-enterprise-security-config with ARI Asset/IP/Mac/User discovery sources only.

cloud_matrix_rows.app_name: Splunk Asset and Risk Intelligence Technical Add-on For Windows
cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-asset-risk-intelligence-setup` Windows TA handoff
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: Cloud app request or Splunkbase app 7214 for indexer-side package placement; Universal Forwarder deployment remains customer-managed
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Validate ari_ta index readiness and hand off local inputs.conf deployment plus ARI data source activation for Asset, Software, and optional Encryption sources
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: ARI Technical Add-on for Windows endpoint discovery. Deploy to indexers without local config and to Universal Forwarders with local inputs.conf.

cloud_matrix_rows.app_name: Splunk Asset and Risk Intelligence Technical Add-on For Linux
cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-asset-risk-intelligence-setup` Linux TA handoff
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: Cloud app request or Splunkbase app 7416 for indexer-side package placement; Universal Forwarder deployment remains customer-managed
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Validate ari_ta index readiness and hand off local inputs.conf deployment plus ARI data source activation for Asset and optional Encryption sources
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: ARI Technical Add-on for Linux endpoint discovery. Deploy to indexers without local config and to Universal Forwarders with local inputs.conf.

cloud_matrix_rows.app_name: Splunk Asset and Risk Intelligence Technical Add-on For macOS
cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-asset-risk-intelligence-setup` macOS TA handoff
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: Cloud app request or Splunkbase app 7417 for indexer-side package placement; Universal Forwarder deployment remains customer-managed
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Validate ari_ta index readiness and hand off local inputs.conf deployment plus ARI data source activation for Asset, Software, and optional Encryption sources
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: ARI Technical Add-on for macOS endpoint discovery. Deploy to indexers without local config and to Universal Forwarders with local inputs.conf.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-attack-analyzer-setup` add-on
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase or local package
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Create/validate saa index and configure tenant/input settings through supported add-on UI or handoff using file-based API key material
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Splunk Add-on for Splunk Attack Analyzer. The setup workflow installs it with the companion dashboard app by default.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-attack-analyzer-setup` app
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase or local package
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Search-tier macro validation for saa_indexes and dashboard readiness
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Splunk App for Splunk Attack Analyzer. The setup workflow defaults saa_indexes to index=saa.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-soar-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Configure SOAR service connections in the app UI using file-based auth material; Enterprise distributed deployments also require indexer-side placement
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Splunk App for SOAR imports SOAR data and remote-search capabilities into the Splunk platform; it does not install a SOAR server.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-soar-setup` export app
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase or Cloud support workflow
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Configure Splunk platform to SOAR server export settings, role alignment, TLS trust, and retry behavior
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Splunk App for SOAR Export is optional and used when Splunk platform data flows to SOAR.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-uba-setup` Kafka app
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase or local package when still required by an existing UBA deployment
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Readiness validation only; standalone UBA server lifecycle remains a manual/professional-services handoff
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Optional legacy UBA Kafka ingestion app. New UEBA work should route to Enterprise Security Premier UEBA.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-enterprise-security-config` content update
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase or ES Cloud managed content workflow
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Configured through the ES content library workflow for ESCU subscription, story/detection enablement, and content-pack toggles
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Splunk ES Content Update is registered under ES configuration, not as a separate security product skill.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-ai-assistant-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: App-local Context, Model Runtime, and feature settings happen in the app UI
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Search-tier app only. Splunk Cloud installs stay on the public Splunkbase path; Enterprise uses cloud-connected activation after install. Latest verified Splunkbase release is 2.0.0; Agent Mode is Cloud-region gated and FedRAMP IL2 support is limited.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-ai-ml-toolkit-setup` AI Toolkit
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase after installing the matching Python for Scientific Computing add-on
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Search-tier validation plus UI handoffs for ML-SPL, Smart Assistants, anomaly workflows, LLM `ai` command providers, Connections, Container Management, ONNX, alerts, safeguards, and model inventory
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Splunk AI Toolkit / MLTK (Splunkbase 2890). Latest audited release is 5.7.3 with Cisco Deep Time Series anomaly detection; live installs intentionally pull the latest compatible Splunkbase release and do not pin --app-version.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-ai-ml-toolkit-setup` PSC Linux 64-bit
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase for Linux search heads; default PSC target for Splunk Cloud
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: No app-local config; validates AI Toolkit Python runtime dependency
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Python for Scientific Computing add-on for Linux 64-bit search heads, Splunkbase 2882, latest audited 4.3.1.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-ai-ml-toolkit-setup` PSC Windows 64-bit
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: Splunkbase install on Windows search heads you control
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: No app-local config; validates AI Toolkit Python runtime dependency
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Python for Scientific Computing add-on for Windows 64-bit search heads, Splunkbase 2883, latest audited 4.3.1.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-ai-ml-toolkit-setup` PSC Mac Intel
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: Splunkbase install on Mac Intel search heads you control
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: No app-local config; validates AI Toolkit Python runtime dependency
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Python for Scientific Computing add-on for Mac Intel search heads, Splunkbase 2881, latest audited 4.3.1.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-ai-ml-toolkit-setup` PSC Mac Apple Silicon
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: Splunkbase install on Mac Apple Silicon search heads you control
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: No app-local config; validates AI Toolkit Python runtime dependency
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Python for Scientific Computing add-on for Mac Apple Silicon search heads, Splunkbase 6785, latest audited 4.3.1.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-ai-ml-toolkit-setup` DSDL
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase after AI Toolkit and PSC prerequisites
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Search-tier setup plus rendered handoffs for Docker, Kubernetes, OpenShift, HPC, GPU, air-gapped images, setup-page/runtime health, API endpoint, HEC/Observability telemetry, Jupyter notebooks, RAG/LLM, and model governance
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Splunk App for Data Science and Deep Learning / DSDL (Splunkbase 4607). External runtimes remain operator-owned handoffs unless a downstream runtime skill applies them.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-mcp-server-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS private app upload or vetted local package
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: REST for mcp.conf settings, token policy, and rate limits; local JSON policy overlays remain package-content concerns
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Search-tier MCP endpoint for Splunk with encrypted token issuance and shared Cursor/Codex/Claude Code bridge rendering.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-agent-management-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: Not a Splunk Cloud install path; renders customer-managed Agent Management assets
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Rendered serverclass.conf, deployment apps, and deploymentclient.conf for Enterprise deployment-server style workflows
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Splunk Enterprise 10.x Agent Management workflow for full Enterprise instances, forwarders, and OTel fleet overview.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-universal-forwarder-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: No Splunk Cloud app install; customer-managed UF runtime forwards to Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Local/SSH UF install and enrollment, rendered Windows MSI bootstrap, or Splunk Cloud Universal Forwarder credentials package install
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Runtime bootstrap workflow for Universal Forwarders. Server classes and deployment apps remain delegated to splunk-agent-management-setup.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-workload-management-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: Not a Splunk Cloud install path; configures self-managed Enterprise Workload Management
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Rendered workload_pools.conf, workload_rules.conf, workload_policy.conf, and local reload helpers
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Linux-only Enterprise workflow for cgroups-backed workload pools, workload rules, and admission-rule guardrails.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-hec-service-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: No Splunk Cloud app install; Splunk Cloud HEC tokens are managed through ACS
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Enterprise inputs.conf rendering or Splunk Cloud ACS HEC token payloads and helper commands
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Reusable HEC platform service workflow for allowed indexes, default source/sourcetype, TLS, and ACK-aware token handling.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-platform-restart-orchestrator`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: No Splunk Cloud app install; plans and executes ACS restarts only when restartRequired=true
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Topology-aware restart/reload planning for Splunk Cloud ACS, Enterprise systemd/CLI paths, deployment-server reloads, SHC rolling restart, and indexer-cluster handoffs
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Repo-wide restart guardrail workflow. Audits restart call sites, avoids default REST restarts on systemd hosts, and renders handoffs when no safe noninteractive restart path exists.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-connect-for-otlp-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase for supported Cloud topologies; customer-managed heavy forwarder fallback when inbound OTLP reachability is not proven
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: REST modular input configuration plus HEC token handoff through splunk-hec-service-setup
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Hybrid-gated OTLP modular input. Victoria direct execution requires topology and inbound reachability validation; Classic routes input execution to IDM or customer-managed heavy forwarder. External collectors and SDKs are senders/clients only.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-admin-doctor`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: No Splunk Cloud app install; diagnoses Splunk Cloud and Enterprise admin posture from evidence and safe local probes
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Rendered doctor, fix-plan, coverage, handoff, and support-ticket packets; Cloud mutations remain delegated to ACS-capable skills
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Full-coverage admin doctor for Cloud and self-managed Enterprise. Classifies every major admin domain as direct local guidance, delegated fix, manual/support path, diagnose-only, or not applicable.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-cloud-data-manager-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: No Splunk Cloud app install; Data Manager is a native Splunk Cloud Platform onboarding experience for cloud-service data
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Render-first readiness, doctor, validation, and supported artifact apply workflow for Data Manager AWS CloudFormation/StackSets, Azure ARM, GCP Terraform templates, CrowdStrike FDR handoffs, HEC/index checks, health searches, and provider prerequisite guardrails
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, and CrowdStrike Data Manager onboarding without claiming private Data Manager APIs or Terraform CRUD for Data Manager inputs. Input creation remains a Data Manager UI handoff; splunk/scp Terraform is adjacent only for supported prerequisites such as indexes, HEC tokens, roles, and allowlists.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-federated-search-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: No Splunk Cloud app install; Federated Search is native platform configuration on the Federated Search Head
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: REST apply path (`apply-rest.sh`) targets Splunk Cloud `/services/data/federated/{provider,index,settings/general}` directly; Splunk Cloud cannot edit federated.conf, so `aws_s3` providers are rendered as REST payloads with AWS Glue/S3/KMS prerequisites docs; admin user must hold `admin_all_objects`
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Complete Federated Search product surface: FSS2S (`type=splunk`) standard or transparent mode with multi-provider/multi-index render, FSS3 (`type=aws_s3`, Splunk Cloud only) REST payloads, file-based and REST apply paths, global federated-search enable/disable, SHC `conf_replication_include.indexes` deployer asset, and live `connectivityStatus` probe.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-index-lifecycle-smartstore-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: Not a Splunk Cloud install path; SmartStore is managed by Splunk Cloud
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Rendered self-managed Enterprise SmartStore indexes.conf, server.conf, and limits.conf assets for indexers or cluster managers
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Self-managed index lifecycle workflow for remote volumes, retention limits, cache manager settings, remote-storage localization limits, and cluster bundle apply.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-monitoring-console-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: Not a Splunk Cloud app install; Cloud Monitoring Console is Splunk-managed
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Rendered self-managed Enterprise Monitoring Console local configuration plus review-only distsearch.conf peer and group helpers
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Distributed or standalone Monitoring Console workflow for auto-config, distributed search groups, forwarder monitoring, and platform alert enablement.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-stream-setup` search-tier app
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS or Splunk Cloud support workflow
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Stream UI / REST on search tier
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Cloud deployment is hybrid, not single-target.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-stream-setup` wire-data add-on
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS or bundled with Stream deployment
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: No special post-install config in normal flow
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Knowledge-object support for Stream search content.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-stream-setup` forwarder add-on
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: Manual install on HF/UF you control
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Local HF files plus host forwarding config
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: This package runs on the heavy/universal forwarder, not the Cloud search tier.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-connect-for-syslog-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: No Splunk Cloud app install; SC4S runtime is rendered for customer-managed hosts or Kubernetes
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: ACS for indexes/HEC where available, search-tier REST for validation, rendered host/Helm assets for runtime
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: External syslog-ng collector pattern. Modeled as a workflow row rather than a Splunk app package.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-connect-for-snmp-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: No Splunk Cloud app install; SC4SNMP runtime is rendered for customer-managed Docker Compose or Kubernetes
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: ACS for indexes/HEC where available, search-tier REST for validation, rendered compose/Helm assets for runtime
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: External SNMP collector pattern. Modeled as a workflow row rather than a Splunk app package.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-observability-otel-collector-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: No Splunk Cloud app install; OTel Collector runtime is rendered for customer-managed Kubernetes clusters or Linux hosts
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Rendered Helm values, Kubernetes secret helpers, Linux installer wrappers, and optional HEC token handoff scripts using file-based tokens
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: External OpenTelemetry Collector workflow for Observability metrics, traces, profiling, Kubernetes events, discovery, auto-instrumentation, and optional Splunk Platform HEC logs with splunk-hec-service-setup handoff helpers.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-observability-ai-agent-monitoring-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: No Splunk Cloud app install; AI Agent Monitoring uses customer-managed Python instrumentation, the Splunk OTel Collector, Splunk Platform HEC, and Log Observer Connect prerequisites
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Rendered GenAI package/runtime env plans, histogram collector overlays, HEC and Log Observer Connect handoffs, dashboard/detector specs, and an explicit coverage report for every AI Agent Monitoring and AI Infrastructure Monitoring surface
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Render-first orchestration skill for Splunk AI Agent Monitoring. Delegates collector apply to splunk-observability-otel-collector-setup, HEC apply to splunk-hec-service-setup, LOC apply to splunk-observability-cloud-integration-setup, dashboards to splunk-observability-dashboard-builder, detectors to splunk-observability-native-ops, and AI infrastructure products to dedicated skills where available. UI-only AI Agent Monitoring connection/index selection remains deeplink/handoff.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-observability-database-monitoring-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: No Splunk Cloud app install; DBMon uses the customer-managed Splunk OTel Collector runtime rendered by splunk-observability-otel-collector-setup
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Rendered Kubernetes clusterReceiver overlay, Linux collector fragment, placeholder-only DB credential Secret stubs, and gateway-routing reference for Splunk Database Monitoring events
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Render/validate-first Splunk Database Monitoring workflow for PostgreSQL, Microsoft SQL Server, and Oracle Database. Enforces DBMon realm availability, collector version floors, official support matrix, clusterReceiver placement, logs/dbmon event exporter, and file/env/Secret-only credential handling.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-observability-aws-integration`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: No Splunk Cloud app install; AWSCloudWatch integration is configured through Splunk Observability Cloud APIs, CloudFormation, Terraform, and AWS-side IAM/Metric Streams resources
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Rendered REST payloads, IAM policy JSON, CloudFormation and StackSets stubs, Terraform configuration, drift reports, PrivateLink notes, and handoff runbooks for adjacent AWS telemetry paths
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Standalone reusable Splunk Observability Cloud AWS integration workflow for polling, Splunk-managed Metric Streams, AWS-managed Metric Streams, and Terraform. Enforces canonical AWSCloudWatch field conflicts, file-based token handling, region and realm guardrails, recommended-stats selection, multi-account StackSets emission, and Splunk_TA_AWS handoff for AWS logs.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-observability-aws-lambda-apm-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: No Splunk Cloud app install; the Splunk OTel Lambda layer (signalfx/splunk-otel-lambda, beta, publisher 254067382080) is attached directly to AWS Lambda functions via AWS CLI, CloudFormation, or Terraform
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Rendered per-function layer ARN attachments, AWS_LAMBDA_EXEC_WRAPPER wiring, SPLUNK_REALM / SPLUNK_ACCESS_TOKEN (resolve references only — Secrets Manager or SSM SecureString), optional OTLP env vars, IAM egress stub (local collector disabled path), Terraform and CloudFormation snippets, and handoff scripts to splunk-observability-dashboard-builder and splunk-observability-native-ops
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Render-first APM instrumentation skill for AWS Lambda via the Splunk OpenTelemetry Lambda layer. Covers Node.js 18/20/22, Python 3.9-3.13, Java 8/11/17/21 on x86_64 and arm64. Per-runtime AWS_LAMBDA_EXEC_WRAPPER wiring; secret-safe SPLUNK_ACCESS_TOKEN delivery via AWS Secrets Manager or SSM SecureString resolve references; layer ARN baked snapshot with opt-in live refresh; vendor/ADOT conflict detection; X-Ray coexistence; GovCloud/China refusal; beta gate. Fulfills the handoffs.lambda_apm stub in splunk-observability-aws-integration.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-observability-k8s-auto-instrumentation-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: No Splunk Cloud app install; overlay on splunk-observability-otel-collector-setup that renders Instrumentation CRs, annotation patches, and the optional OBI DaemonSet for customer-managed Kubernetes clusters
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Rendered Instrumentation CR manifests (per-language images + env), namespace + workload strategic-merge-patches targeting spec.template.metadata.annotations, annotation backup ConfigMap for clean revert, OpenShift SCC stub when OBI is enabled, and idempotent apply + uninstall helper scripts
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Zero-code application auto-instrumentation for Java / Node.js / Python / .NET / Go / Apache / Nginx / SDK workloads. Overlay on splunk-observability-otel-collector-setup (which installs the operator + CRDs). Includes AlwaysOn Profiling + runtime metrics wiring, propagator + sampler configuration, multi-CR support behind the operator multi-instrumentation gate, vendor-coexistence detection, --discover-workloads helper, selective --target apply/uninstall, and --gitops-mode YAML-only rendering.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-observability-k8s-frontend-rum-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: No Splunk Cloud app install; standalone reusable skill (RUM beacons direct to rum-ingest.<realm>.observability.splunkcloud.com) that renders Splunk Browser RUM (@splunk/otel-web 2.x) + Session Replay (Splunk recorder) injection assets for customer-managed Kubernetes clusters
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Rendered K8s manifests for one of four injection modes (nginx pod-side ConfigMap with sub_filter, ingress-nginx configuration-snippet annotation, busybox+emptyDir initContainer rewriter for distroless, runtime-config ConfigMap for npm-bundled SDK), injection-backup ConfigMap, full SplunkRum.init + Frustration Signals 2.0 + Session Replay surfaces, JavaScript source-map upload helper wrapping the splunk-rum CLI + @splunk/rum-build-plugins Webpack plugin, RUM-to-APM Server-Timing trace-linking validation
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Splunk Browser RUM + Session Replay injection. NOT AppDynamics BRUM (handled by splunk-appdynamics-eum-setup). Two distinct credential files: SPLUNK_O11Y_RUM_TOKEN_FILE for the browser-embedded RUM token, SPLUNK_O11Y_TOKEN_FILE reused for the server-to-server source-map upload. Hands off RUM dashboards to splunk-observability-dashboard-builder, RUM detectors to splunk-observability-native-ops, the SIM rum modular input to splunk-observability-cloud-integration-setup, and RUM-to-APM trace linking to splunk-observability-k8s-auto-instrumentation-setup.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-observability-dashboard-builder`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: No Splunk Cloud app install; renders Splunk Observability Cloud dashboard groups, charts, dashboards, and apply plans
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Validate and optionally apply classic Observability dashboard API payloads; access tokens stay file-based and are never placed in chat or command arguments
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Dashboard composition workflow for Observability dashboard groups, charts, variables, detector links, runbooks, rendered API payloads, and optional applies.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-observability-deep-native-workflows`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: No Splunk Cloud app install; renders UI-aware native Observability Cloud workflow packets and API-vs-UI coverage reports
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Render modern dashboard, APM service map/service view/business transaction/trace/profiling, RUM replay/error/URL grouping/mobile, DBMon, Synthetics, SLO, Infrastructure/Kubernetes/Network Explorer, Related Content, AI Assistant, logs chart, and Observability Cloud Mobile app plans with API-vs-UI status, deeplinks, API action plans, and downstream skill handoffs
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Deep native Observability workflow planner for product surfaces that need UI-aware runbooks or public API action plans. Complements dashboard-builder, native-ops, cloud-integration, RUM, DBMon, and OTel collector skills without owning collector runtime placement.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-observability-native-ops`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: No Splunk Cloud app install; renders native Splunk Observability Cloud operations and handoff plans
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Validate and optionally apply Observability API payloads for detectors, teams, alert routing, Synthetics, and APM trace/topology checks; render deeplinks and handoffs for SLOs, RUM, service maps, modern logs charts, and On-Call deeplink handoffs (full On-Call coverage lives in splunk-oncall-setup)
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Native Observability operations workflow; complements OTel collection and classic dashboard rendering without Splunk Platform runtime placement.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-oncall-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: Splunk-side companion apps install through ACS or REST: Splunkbase 3546 (victorops_app, alert action) on a search head or SHC deployer; Splunkbase 4886 (TA-splunk-add-on-for-victorops, public-API ingest) on a heavy forwarder; Splunkbase 5863 (splunkoncall, SOAR connector) installs through Splunk SOAR rather than splunk-app-install
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Render and apply the full Splunk On-Call lifecycle through the public API at api.victorops.com (X-VO-Api-Id + X-VO-Api-Key file-based auth) with per-endpoint rate-limit governors; send REST endpoint and generic email alerts; pre-create the four indexes the Add-on macros expect (victorops_users / victorops_teams / victorops_oncall / victorops_incidents); seed the alert-action mycollection KV-store; toggle the recovery_polling saved search; render ITSI NEAP, ES Adaptive Response, and Observability detector-recipient handoff stubs
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Render-first lifecycle for Splunk On-Call (formerly VictorOps). Splunkbase apps: 3546 (victorops_app), 4886 (TA-splunk-add-on-for-victorops), 5863 (splunkoncall SOAR connector). Supersedes the partial on_call block in splunk-observability-native-ops which now defers via deeplink-only handoff.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-license-manager-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: Not a Splunk Cloud install path; Splunk Cloud licensing is Splunk-managed
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Renders self-managed Enterprise license install commands, REST license group / pool payloads, and per-peer `splunk edit licenser-localpeer` wrappers
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Self-managed Splunk Enterprise license manager / peer / pool / group lifecycle. Co-locates with cluster-manager, MC, deployment server, SHC deployer, search head, or indexer.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-edge-processor-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: No Splunk Cloud app install; Edge Processor runtime is rendered for customer-managed Linux hosts and joined to a Splunk Cloud Platform tenant or Splunk Enterprise 10.0+ data management node
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Renders EP control-plane object (TLS / mTLS), source types, destinations (Splunk S2S, Splunk HEC, Amazon S3, syslog), SPL2 pipelines, apply-objects orchestrator, per-host install (systemd / no-systemd / Docker), and forwarder outputs.conf
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Default-destination guard prevents silent data loss. Emits ACS allowlist hand-off stub for s2s + hec features when destinations target Splunk Cloud.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-indexer-cluster-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: Not a Splunk Cloud install path; Splunk Cloud indexer clusters are Splunk-managed
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Renders self-managed Enterprise single-site or multisite cluster manager, peer, and SH server.conf snippets; sequenced bootstrap; cluster bundle validate / status / apply / rollback; rolling restart (default / searchable / forced); peer offline (fast / enforce-counts); maintenance mode; manager redundancy (HAProxy + DNS); migration to multisite; non-clustered-to-clustered migration
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Sits above splunk-enterprise-host-setup (per-host install). Emits a license-peers hand-off stub for splunk-license-manager-setup.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-cloud-acs-allowlist-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: No Splunk Cloud app install; manages Splunk Cloud ACS IP allowlists for all 7 ACS features
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Renders desired-state plan for acs / search-api / hec / s2s / search-ui / idm-api / idm-ui (IPv4 + IPv6); preflight enforces AWS 200/feature + 230/group and GCP 200/feature limits; lock-out protection refuses acs feature edits without operator IP; status polling on /adminconfig/v2/status; optional terraform-snippets.tf via splunk/scp
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Explicit user-driven counterpart to acs_helpers.sh acs_ensure_search_api_access. FedRAMP High excluded (Splunk Support only).

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-enterprise-host-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: Not a Splunk Cloud install path; bootstraps customer-managed Enterprise hosts only
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Local or SSH-driven Linux host bootstrap for search-tier, indexer, and heavy-forwarder roles
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Enterprise runtime bootstrap workflow for standalone or single-site clustered hosts.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-enterprise-kubernetes-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: Not a Splunk Cloud install path; renders customer-managed Splunk Enterprise Kubernetes deployments
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Rendered SOK Helm assets or Splunk POD cluster-config.yaml and installer helpers
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Self-managed Kubernetes runtime workflow for SOK S1/C3/M4 or Splunk POD on Cisco UCS.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `cisco-spaces-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: REST via UCC custom stream/input handlers
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Cisco EULA license-ack required. Firehose streaming input for Cisco Spaces indoor location analytics.

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-observability-cloud-integration-setup`
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: No Splunk Cloud app install of its own; orchestrates pairing + Discover-app + LOC + SIM Add-on (Splunkbase 5247) + cross-skill handoffs to splunk-cloud-acs-allowlist-setup, splunk-app-install, and splunk-itsi-config
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: Render-first integration setup: token-auth flip, Unified Identity or Service Account pairing (acs observability + /adminconfig/v2/observability/sso-pairing), multi-org default-org deeplink, Centralized RBAC (enable-capabilities + enable-centralized-rbac), Discover Splunk Observability Cloud app five Configurations tabs, Log Observer Connect service-account user/role/workload rule, Related Content + Real Time Metrics capability assignments, Dashboard Studio O11y metrics, Splunk Infrastructure Monitoring Add-on account + curated SignalFlow modular inputs with MTS sizing preflight; file-based tokens only, --i-accept-rbac-cutover guard for the destructive RBAC step
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Single skill that turns a Splunk Cloud Platform or Splunk Enterprise stack into a fully-paired Splunk Observability Cloud experience. Uses Splunk_TA_sim (Splunkbase 5247) for the in-Splunk navigation surface (sim SPL command + streaming modular inputs).

cloud_matrix_rows.label: `splunk-observability-cloud-integration-setup` (SIM Add-on)
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_install_path: ACS Splunkbase (id 5247); install on search heads + Inputs Data Manager
cloud_matrix_rows.cloud_config_path: REST via UCC custom account + modular-input handlers under /servicesNS/nobody/Splunk_TA_sim
cloud_matrix_rows.notes: Splunk Infrastructure Monitoring Add-on. Provides the sim SPL command and the splunk_infrastructure_monitoring_data_streams modular input. Hard cap: 250,000 MTS per modular input. Splunk Synthetic Monitoring Add-on (Splunkbase 5608) is archived (deprecated) and replaced by SIM streams.

skill_topologies.notes: API-driven collector app; run on the search tier or a heavy forwarder you control.
Forwarder-side field-mapping add-on for NetFlow or IPFIX receiver paths.
Collector and dashboard setup skill; use on the search tier or a heavy forwarder.
Product-specific Cisco Security collector app; compatible with the search tier or a heavy forwarder.
Secure Access onboarding and dashboard setup app; place on the search tier or a heavy forwarder.
Collector app for ACI, Nexus Dashboard, and Nexus 9K data; use on the search tier or a heavy forwarder.
Search-time visualization app with macros, saved searches, and KV Store content.
API collector app; run on the search tier or a heavy forwarder.
Meraki collector app with dashboard alignment; place on the search tier or a heavy forwarder.
Search-head Webex REST API collection and dashboard setup for the Webex Add-on plus Webex App.
Cisco UCS Manager API collection through Splunk_TA_cisco-ucs server/template/task handlers.
Cisco ESA/WSA parser and ingestion-handoff workflow. Use SC4S or file monitors for collection; install TAs on search and parsing tiers.
Enterprise Security Cloud-first Talos reputation integration with custom REST handler, adaptive response actions, and service-account readiness validation.
Product-name router that resolves a Cisco product from the SCAN catalog and delegates to the matching setup skill or explicit gap classification.
Hybrid-capable app that combines HEC push with polling and dashboard setup.
Generic package delivery skill; actual package compatibility comes from the package or app metadata.

Splunkbase app 8704 modular input runs on search-tier or heavy-forwarder only after topology checks; external collectors and SDKs are OTLP senders/clients, not app install targets. Classic Cloud routes to IDM or customer-managed heavy forwarder.
External collector workflow that prepares Splunk-side objects and renders SC4S runtime assets.
External collector workflow that prepares Splunk-side objects and renders SC4SNMP runtime assets.

External OTel Collector workflow for Kubernetes and Linux runtimes that send data to Splunk Observability Cloud and optional Splunk Platform HEC, with HEC token handoff helpers delegated to splunk-hec-service-setup.

Render-first orchestration for Splunk AI Agent Monitoring and AI Infrastructure Monitoring. Requires external collector runtime for traces/metrics/logs, Splunk Platform HEC and Log Observer Connect prerequisites for evaluation/content event correlation, and delegates dashboards/detectors to native Observability skills. UI-only AI Agent Monitoring connection/index selection remains a deeplink handoff.

Render/validate-first Splunk Database Monitoring workflow for PostgreSQL, Microsoft SQL Server, and Oracle Database. Renders Splunk OTel Collector Kubernetes clusterReceiver overlays and Linux collector fragments, validates official DBMon realm/platform/version support, and keeps all credentials in Kubernetes Secrets or local environment variables.

Overlay on splunk-observability-otel-collector-setup. Renders per-language OpenTelemetry Operator Instrumentation CRs, workload annotation strategic-merge patches with backup/restore, OBI eBPF DaemonSet + OpenShift SCC, AlwaysOn Profiling + runtime metrics, propagator/sampler, multi-CR, Fargate gateway endpoint, vendor-coexistence detection, --discover-workloads inventory, selective --target apply/uninstall, and --gitops-mode. Never takes tokens; inherits ingest-token handling from the base collector.

Standalone reusable skill for Splunk Browser RUM (@splunk/otel-web 2.x) + Session Replay (Splunk recorder) injection into Kubernetes-served frontends. RUM beacons land directly at rum-ingest.<realm>.observability.splunkcloud.com -- no OTel collector required for browser telemetry. Four injection modes (nginx pod-side ConfigMap with sub_filter, ingress-nginx configuration-snippet annotation, busybox+emptyDir initContainer rewriter for distroless, runtime-config ConfigMap for npm-bundled SDK), full Frustration Signals 2.0 (rage / dead / error / thrashed cursor) and Session Replay (sensitivityRules + features.{canvas,video,iframes,packAssets,cacheAssets,backgroundServiceSrc} + sampler) gated behind --accept-session-replay-enterprise, JavaScript source-map upload helper wrapping the splunk-rum CLI + @splunk/rum-build-plugins Webpack plugin (reuses SPLUNK_O11Y_TOKEN_FILE), RUM-to-APM Server-Timing trace-linking validation, multi-workload spec, distroless image auto-detection, ingress-nginx CVE-2021-25742 preflight, version-pin enforcement, SRI hash emission, and --gitops-mode YAML-only rendering. Hands off RUM dashboards to splunk-observability-dashboard-builder, RUM detectors to splunk-observability-native-ops, the SIM rum modular input to splunk-observability-cloud-integration-setup, and RUM-to-APM trace linking to splunk-observability-k8s-auto-instrumentation-setup. Two credential files: SPLUNK_O11Y_RUM_TOKEN_FILE for the browser-embedded RUM token, SPLUNK_O11Y_TOKEN_FILE for the server-to-server source-map upload. Explicitly NOT AppDynamics BRUM.
Splunk Observability Cloud dashboard render/apply workflow; no Splunk Platform runtime placement.

Deep native Observability Cloud workflow planner for modern dashboards, APM service maps/service views/business transactions/trace waterfalls/profiling, RUM session replay/error analysis/URL grouping/mobile RUM, DBMon explain-plan triage, Synthetic waterfalls, SLOs, Infrastructure/Kubernetes/Network Explorer, Related Content, AI Assistant, logs charts, and Observability Cloud Mobile app handoffs. No Splunk Platform runtime placement.
Splunk Observability Cloud native operations render/apply workflow; no Splunk Platform runtime placement.

Splunk Platform <-> Splunk Observability Cloud pairing + Discover app + Log Observer Connect + Related Content + Real Time Metrics + Splunk Infrastructure Monitoring Add-on (Splunkbase 5247). Search-tier required; heavy-forwarder optional for ITSI / SIM modular-input streaming. Hands off Splunkbase install to splunk-app-install, ACS allowlist deltas to splunk-cloud-acs-allowlist-setup, and the Content Pack to splunk-itsi-config.

Splunk On-Call (formerly VictorOps) lifecycle. Splunkbase 3546 (victorops_app) installs on a search head or SHC deployer; Splunkbase 4886 (TA-splunk-add-on-for-victorops) installs on a heavy forwarder and creates inputs for users, teams, on-call schedules, and incidents; Splunkbase 5863 (splunkoncall) is a SOAR connector. The dedicated `splunk-oncall-setup` skill is the canonical home for all Splunk On-Call work.
Agent Management control-plane workflow for server classes, deployment apps, and deployment clients; do not target indexer cluster peers or SHC members directly.
Customer-managed Universal Forwarder runtime bootstrap and enrollment workflow; complements Agent Management server-class and deployment-app workflows.
Self-managed Enterprise Workload Management workflow for Linux search heads and indexers.
Reusable HEC token and service configuration workflow for Enterprise HEC tiers or Splunk Cloud ACS-managed HEC.

Splunk Platform restart and reload orchestration workflow. Covers Cloud ACS restart checks, Enterprise systemd/CLI/REST fallback decisions, deployment-server reloads, SHC/IDXC handoffs, and repo-wide restart audit reports.

Admin diagnosis and routing workflow for Splunk Cloud and self-managed Enterprise. Renders local doctor/fix/coverage packets and delegates specialized remediation to existing skills; it is not a Splunk runtime package.

Splunk Cloud Platform Data Manager readiness and provider-artifact workflow. Requires the Splunk Cloud primary search head for Data Manager UI/readiness and supports customer-managed cloud-provider artifacts for AWS CloudFormation/StackSets, Azure ARM, GCP Terraform, and CrowdStrike FDR S3/SQS handoffs.

Federated Search Head workflow covering Federated Search for Splunk (FSS2S, type=splunk) in standard or transparent mode and Federated Search for Amazon S3 (FSS3, type=aws_s3, Splunk Cloud only). Renders multi-provider/multi-index assets, file-based apply for Enterprise SHs and SHC deployers, REST apply for both Enterprise and Splunk Cloud, the global federated-search enable/disable switch, and a connectivityStatus probe.
Self-managed indexer workflow for SmartStore remote volumes, retention policy, cache manager settings, and low-level remote-storage localization limits.
Search-tier Monitoring Console workflow for distributed or standalone mode, distributed search groups, forwarder monitoring, platform alerts, and peer status review.
Linux host bootstrap workflow for self-managed Splunk Enterprise search tiers, indexers, cluster managers, SHC members, and heavy forwarders.
Self-managed Splunk Enterprise Kubernetes runtime workflow using SOK Helm assets or Splunk POD installer assets.

On-prem Splunk Enterprise public-internet exposure hardening workflow for Splunk Web, HEC, S2S, and splunkd REST edge surfaces. Renders Splunk-side overlays plus reverse-proxy, WAF, firewall, and operator handoff assets; Cloud is not a target.

On-prem Splunk Enterprise PKI lifecycle for every TLS surface (Splunk Web 8000, splunkd REST 8089, S2S 9997, HEC 8088, KV Store, indexer cluster including replication_port-ssl 9887, SHC, License Manager, Deployment Server, Monitoring Console, Federated Search, DMZ heavy forwarder, UF fleet, Edge Processor RSA + ECDSA, SAML SP signing, LDAPS trust, splunk CLI cacert.pem). Three TLS algorithm presets (splunk-modern, fips-140-3, stig); FIPS 140-2 / 140-3 wiring via splunk-launch.conf. Cloud is not a target (Splunk Cloud manages its own TLS).
Premium search-tier app for ITSI dashboards and service analytics.
Search-tier workflow for ITSI content-pack bootstrap, validation, and service-topology automation.
Premium search-tier SIEM app install and essinstall workflow for standalone search heads or SHC deployers.
ES operational configuration workflow for search-tier objects and index-tier ES index readiness.
Security product router/audit workflow that resolves product names and delegates to first-class setup skills, existing ES workflows, generic install-only routes, or explicit handoff classifications.
Search-head-only Splunk Security Essentials app; no indexer, forwarder, or collector placement.

Splunk platform-side SOAR integration apps belong on search heads and, for Enterprise distributed deployments, indexers; Automation Broker and SOAR On-prem servers are represented as external-collector handoffs.
Self-managed Splunk Enterprise license manager and license peer workflow. Co-locates with cluster manager, MC, deployment server, SHC deployer, search head, or indexer.

Self-managed Splunk Enterprise indexer cluster bootstrap and operations workflow. Sits above splunk-enterprise-host-setup (per-host install) and emits a license-peers handoff stub for splunk-license-manager-setup.

Splunk Edge Processor instances run as a customer-managed external collector tier (Linux, systemd / no-systemd / Docker) joined to a Splunk Cloud Platform tenant or Splunk Enterprise 10.0+ data management node. Emits an ACS allowlist handoff stub for s2s + hec features when destinations target Splunk Cloud.
Splunk Cloud ACS IP allowlist control-plane workflow; manages all 7 ACS features (acs / search-api / hec / s2s / search-ui / idm-api / idm-ui) for IPv4 + IPv6. No Splunk Enterprise role placement.
Readiness and migration workflow for existing UBA/UEBA deployments; standalone UBA server installation is not automated, and optional Kafka app placement is search-tier focused.
Installs the Splunk Attack Analyzer add-on and dashboard app, creates/validates the saa index, and aligns the saa_indexes macro; API credentials remain file-based handoff material.

Restricted ARI app with search-tier UI/KV Store requirements, index-tier readiness for ari_staging, ari_asset, ari_internal, and ari_ta, read-only validation, and lifecycle-complete handoffs for post-install, data sources, metrics, responses/audit, investigations, ES integration, Exposure Analytics, Add-ons, Echo, upgrade, and teardown prerequisites.
Search-tier Splunk AI Assistant app with cloud-backed inference, Context and Model Runtime app UI settings, Agent Mode Cloud-region gating, and KV Store-backed local chat state.

Search-tier AI Toolkit/MLTK, PSC, and optional DSDL app workflow with external runtime handoffs for Docker, Kubernetes, OpenShift, HPC, GPU, air-gapped images, LLM providers, Connections, Container Management, DSDL API/health/telemetry, and legacy anomaly migration.
Search-tier MCP service app with custom REST handlers, KV Store-backed tool metadata, encrypted token issuance, and optional local policy overlays.
Split-package Stream deployment: search-tier UI, forwarder-side capture, and optional indexer knowledge objects.
Cisco Spaces firehose collector app with UCC REST handlers for stream configuration and activation token encryption.
Search-head-only catalog and management app. No data ingestion — provides product catalog UI, ecosystem intelligence, and Splunkbase analytics.
Cisco ThousandEyes MCP Server bridge for Cursor / Claude / Codex / VS Code / AWS Kiro AI assistants. No Splunk Platform runtime placement; operator-side MCP wiring only.

Splunk Observability Cloud <-> ThousandEyes integration via Streams API v7 (OTLP metrics) + Integrations 2.0 APM connector + full TE asset lifecycle (tests, alert rules, labels, tags, dashboards, templates). No Splunk Platform runtime placement.

Galileo SaaS/Enterprise readiness, object lifecycle provisioning for projects/log streams/datasets/prompts/experiments/metrics/Protect stages/Agent Control targets, full Galileo feature coverage handoffs for REST API/custom deployments, SSO/RBAC, Luna-2, scorers, workflows, tags/metadata, enterprise retention/TTL/privacy, Agent Graph and console debugging, MCP tool calls, cookbooks, async jobs, and release compatibility, plus Splunk workflow orchestration: export_records to Splunk HEC, Observe OpenTelemetry/OpenInference snippets, Protect invoke snippets, Evaluate annotation/feedback handoffs, Splunk Connect for OTLP handoff, Splunk OTel Collector handoff, and dashboard/detector handoffs. Use --o11y-only for Splunk Observability Cloud-only mode with no Splunk Platform HEC dependency.

Agent Control runtime governance workflow: server/auth/control templates, Python/TypeScript runtime snippets, OTel sink config, custom Splunk HEC event sink, Splunk HEC service handoff, Splunk OTel Collector handoff, and dashboard/detector handoffs.

Isovalent platform installation lifecycle for Cilium, Hubble, and Tetragon (OSS or Enterprise editions). Renders Helm values and install/upgrade scripts; runtime targets Kubernetes, not Splunk Platform.

Splunk Observability Cloud + Splunk Platform integration for Isovalent (Cilium, Hubble, Tetragon). Renders OTel collector overlay (seven prometheus/isovalent_* scrape jobs + filter/includemetrics) plus file-based Tetragon log path via OTel filelog receiver and hostPath mount. Optional handoffs to splunk-hec-service-setup, splunk-observability-dashboard-builder, splunk-observability-native-ops, and cisco-security-cloud-setup.

Splunk Observability Cloud integration for Cisco Nexus (and any cisco_os receiver-supported NX-OS / IOS-XE / IOS-XR device) metrics via the OTel cisco_os receiver in the multi-device + global-scrapers format (PR #45562, contrib v0.149.0+). Renders clusterReceiver overlay, K8s Secret manifest stub for SSH credentials, dashboards, and starter detectors.

Splunk Observability Cloud integration for Cisco Intersight (UCS management plane) metrics via the cisco_intersight OTel receiver. Renders a separate intersight-otel namespace, K8s Secret manifest stub for the Intersight OAuth2 client, Deployment + ConfigMap shipping OTLP gRPC to the main Splunk OTel collector agent (port 4317), and dashboards / detectors.

Splunk Observability Cloud integration for NVIDIA GPU telemetry (DCGM Exporter). Renders receiver_creator/dcgm-cisco (custom name to avoid the chart's autodetect/nvidia collision), dual-label discovery rule, dual-pipeline filtering, and the optional --enable-dcgm-pod-labels patch (RBAC + DaemonSet env patch) to fix the GPU Operator's pod-label gap.

Umbrella skill composing splunk-observability-cisco-nexus-integration, splunk-observability-cisco-intersight-integration, and splunk-observability-nvidia-gpu-integration via subprocess + Python deep-merge, then adding AI-Pod-specific blocks: NIM / vLLM / Milvus / Trident / Portworx / Redfish scrapes, dual-pipeline filtering, k8s_attributes/nim, OpenShift defaults, rbac.customRules (when --nim-scrape-mode endpoints), --workshop-mode multi-tenant pattern, and the OpenShift SCC helper.

Splunk Observability Cloud <-> AWS integration end-to-end across all four documented connection paths: polling, Splunk-managed Metric Streams, AWS-managed Metric Streams, and Terraform. Renders the full canonical AWSCloudWatch field set (POST/PUT /v2/integration with X-SF-Token), per-use-case IAM JSON (foundation, polling, streams, tag sync, Cassandra special-case ARN list), regional or StackSets CloudFormation stubs from signalfx/aws-cloudformation-templates, Splunk-side Terraform pinned to signalfx provider ~> 9.0, conflict-matrix enforcement (services vs namespaceSyncRules, customCloudWatchNamespaces vs customNamespaceSyncRules, metricStreamsManagedExternally requires useMetricStreamsSync), AWS-recommended-stats catalog via collectOnlyRecommendedStats, AWS PrivateLink ingest stubs (default legacy signalfx.com domain), multi-account / AWS Organizations StackSets, drift detection with --quickstart-from-live, and the documented troubleshooting catalog. Live-validated against the operator's existing us1 integration. Hand-offs to splunk-app-install for Splunk_TA_AWS (Splunkbase 1876, min v8.1.1) for AWS log ingestion, splunk-observability-cloud-integration-setup for Log Observer Connect, splunk-observability-dashboard-builder, splunk-observability-native-ops, splunk-observability-otel-collector-setup for EC2/EKS host telemetry, and splunk-observability-aws-lambda-apm-setup for Lambda APM.

Render-first APM instrumentation for AWS Lambda via the Splunk OpenTelemetry Lambda layer (signalfx/splunk-otel-lambda, beta, publisher 254067382080). Covers Node.js 18/20/22, Python 3.9-3.13, Java 8/11/17/21 on x86_64 and arm64. Per-runtime AWS_LAMBDA_EXEC_WRAPPER wiring; secret-safe SPLUNK_ACCESS_TOKEN delivery via AWS Secrets Manager or SSM SecureString resolve references (token value never in files or argv); baked layer ARN snapshot with opt-in live refresh; vendor/ADOT conflict detection; X-Ray coexistence flag; GovCloud/China refusal; IAM egress stub for local-collector-disabled path; AWS CLI / Terraform / CloudFormation variants; rollback, discover-functions, doctor, and cross-skill handoffs. Fulfills the handoffs.lambda_apm stub in splunk-observability-aws-integration.

Splunk Observability Cloud Azure Monitor integration workflow. Renders and optionally applies cloud-side Azure integration payloads, Terraform, Azure CLI and Bicep handoffs, plus dashboard/detector and Splunk Platform log-ingestion add-on handoffs. No Splunk Platform runtime placement for the Observability integration itself.

Splunk Observability Cloud GCP Cloud Monitoring integration workflow. Renders and optionally applies cloud-side GCP integration payloads, Terraform, gcloud Service Account or WIF handoffs, plus dashboard/detector and Splunk Platform log-ingestion add-on handoffs. No Splunk Platform runtime placement for the Observability integration itself.

Self-managed Splunk Enterprise Deployment Server runtime workflow. Bootstraps and operates deployment-server state, phoneHome tuning, REST fleet inspection, HA pairing, reloads, and client migration; composes with splunk-agent-management-setup for serverclass/deployment-app authoring and splunk-universal-forwarder-setup for client enrollment.

Self-managed Splunk Enterprise Search Head Cluster orchestration workflow covering deployer bootstrap, member initialization, bundle validation/apply/rollback, captain transfer, rolling restarts, KV Store health, member add/remove, migrations, and failure-mode runbooks.

AppDynamics suite router and coverage doctor; enforces the checked-in taxonomy for SaaS, On-Premises, release/reference, product-announcement, API, security, AI, SAP, and Splunk Platform paths, and delegates product or Splunk Platform mutations to child skills.

External AppDynamics platform workflow covering On-Premises overview, release/reference review, deployment planning, platform quickstart, Controller, Events Service, EUM Server, Synthetic Server, Enterprise Console, Virtual Appliance, HA, upgrade, and secure platform runbooks; no Splunk app placement.

External AppDynamics Controller admin workflow covering API clients, OAuth, RBAC, SAML/LDAP, permissions, licensing, license rules, sensitive data controls, and tenant admin validation; no Splunk app placement.

External AppDynamics Smart Agent workflow covering remote install/upgrade/rollback, software download/package validation, and managed agent lifecycle; host execution is gated and no Splunk app placement is required.

External AppDynamics APM workflow covering application model, business transactions, service endpoints, snapshots, metrics, serverless monitoring, and Splunk AppDynamics for OpenTelemetry; runtime installs delegate to AppDynamics agent owner skills.
External Kubernetes/AppDynamics workflow; Kubernetes rollout is gated and no Splunk app placement is required.

External AppDynamics Infrastructure Visibility workflow covering Machine Agent, Server Visibility, Network Visibility, Docker/container visibility, service availability, tags, GPU monitoring, Prometheus extension, and infrastructure health rules; no Splunk app placement.
External AppDynamics Database Visibility workflow; no Splunk app placement.
External AppDynamics Analytics workflow; Events API publishing is gated and no Splunk app placement is required.
External AppDynamics EUM workflow; source edits are gated and no Splunk app placement is required.
External AppDynamics Synthetic Monitoring workflow; private agent rollout is rendered and no Splunk app placement is required.
AppDynamics LOC workflow; delegates Splunk service-account and platform operations to Splunk Platform skills.

External AppDynamics alerting content workflow covering health rules, policies, actions, schedules, email digests, suppression, import/export, rollback, anomaly detection, AIML baselines, and automated diagnostics; no Splunk app placement.

External AppDynamics dashboard/report workflow covering custom dashboards, Dash Studio handoffs, ThousandEyes dashboard integration, reports, scheduled reports, War Rooms, and validation; no Splunk app placement.
External AppDynamics tag and extension workflow; no Splunk app placement.

External AppDynamics security and AI workflow covering Application Security Monitoring, Secure Application policies/APIs, Secure Application for OTel Java, Observability for AI, GenAI, GPU, and Cisco AI Pod handoffs.

External AppDynamics SAP Agent workflow covering SAP Agent, ABAP Agent, HTTP SDK, SNP CrystalBridge, BiQ Collector, NetWeaver transports, authorization, release-note, compatibility, and validation runbooks; SAP changes are rendered as runbooks.

apps.label: Splunk AI Toolkit
apps.notes: Splunk AI Toolkit / MLTK app. Requires the PSC add-on matching the search-head OS; supports ML-SPL, Smart Assistants, anomaly workflows, LLM ai command provider handoffs, ONNX, alerts, and model inventory.

apps.label: Splunk App for Data Science and Deep Learning
apps.notes: DSDL extends AI Toolkit with external Docker, Kubernetes, OpenShift, HPC, GPU, air-gapped image, Jupyter notebook, RAG/LLM, and model governance workflows. Runtime setup is rendered as a handoff by default.

apps.label: Splunk Connect for OTLP
apps.notes: OTLP gRPC/HTTP modular input backed by HEC token authentication. Audited package contains Linux x86_64 and Windows x86_64 binaries only, no Darwin/default-bin executable, no dashboards, no setup page, no KV Store, no custom REST handlers, and no Python runtime. External-collector role means sender/client configuration only.

apps.label: Splunk Infrastructure Monitoring Add-on
apps.notes: Provides the sim SPL command + splunk_infrastructure_monitoring_data_streams modular input. Splunk Synthetic Monitoring Add-on (Splunkbase 5608) is archived and replaced by SIM streams; treat that legacy app as deprecated and do not install it.

apps.label: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services
apps.notes: Azure log ingestion TA (Activity Logs, Audit Logs, Azure AD Sign-in Logs, Azure Monitor Logs). Azure Monitor metrics go through the Splunk Observability Azure integration; logs go through this TA. Hand-off from splunk-observability-azure-integration.

apps.label: Microsoft Azure App for Splunk
apps.notes: Azure dashboards for Splunk Platform. Hand-off from splunk-observability-azure-integration (handoffs.microsoft_azure_app=true).

apps.label: Splunk Add-on for Google Cloud Platform
apps.notes: GCP log ingestion TA (Pub/Sub, Cloud Storage, Cloud Audit Logs). GCP metrics go through the Splunk Observability GCP integration; logs go through this TA. Hand-off from splunk-observability-gcp-integration.
